data.title: UAE's Palm Sports to Provide Multi-martial Arts Training Services to Government Institution
data.description:
International Holding (ADX: IHC) subsidiary Palms Sports (ADX:PALMS) secured a new deal worth 40 million Emirati dirhams to provide multi-martial arts training services to an undisclosed government entity.

Under the two-year contract, the Abu Dhabi-based sports management company will assign instructors to meet the requirements of the institution's workforce, according to a Wednesday release.

data.description:
** Shares of travel firm Thomas Cook gain 5.7% to 126.23 rupees

** THOM, with group company STOC Travel, signed a deal with Korea Tourism Organisation

** We aim to enhance engagement for South Korea as a premier travel destination for Indian travellers, Korea Tourism executive said

** Year-to-date, THOM down ~36%, peers Yatra Online and Easy Trip Planners fall 32%, 20%

data.title: Shanghai Electric Power Raises 3.8 Billion Yuan to Repay Debts, Loans
data.description:
Shanghai Electric Power raised 3.8 billion yuan via the issue of its first and second ultra-short-term financing bonds for 2025.

Proceeds will mainly be used to repay maturing debts and settle financial institution loans, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The 86-day bond raised 1.7 billion yuan, while the 149-day bond raised 2.1 billion yuan. Interest rates were 1.98% and 1.96%, respectively.

Shares of the power generation company were down less than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.4% at 75,640, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 0.3% to 22,865, tracking their Asian peers, as uncertainty over U.S. tariff measures dented investor sentiment.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose about 0.2% to 86.8050 per U.S. dollar, tracking a rise in the Chinese yuan and other regional peers, after U.S. President Donald Trump said a new trade deal with Beijing was possible.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.745 rupees, with the yield little changed at 6.6823%, ahead of a large debt purchase by the central bank later in the day.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down slightly at 6.2825%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was little changed at 6.0575%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.40%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.34%.

data.title: CECEP Solar Energy's Units to Trim Electricity Settlement Price of Photovoltaic Project
data.description:
CECEP Solar Energy's units agreed to lower the settlement price for the operation of a rooftop distributed photovoltaic power generation project, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

CEC Hubei Solar Technology had agreed to develop CEC (Jingmen) Environmental Science Water Technology Development's 2-megawatt rooftop photovoltaic power project in 2024.

In the amended deal, the settlement electricity price is no longer 0.68 yuan per kilowatt hour but will be lowered to 0.5 yuan/kWh, which is based on CEC Jingmen's quarterly electricity bill, the solar panel manufacturer said.

data.title: Rising costs, weak demand hit small, midcap Q3 earnings
data.description:
After outperforming large caps for most of FY24, Small and MidCap profits have now started to lag behind for the second consecutive quarter. This underperformance is largely attributed to a domestic slowdown, to which small and midcaps are more exposed, experts said.

According to Moneycontrol data, an aggregate of 683 firms in the BSE SmallCap Index reported a 7.1 percent increase in revenue for Q3, marking the seventh consecutive quarter of single-digit growth and the 13th straight quarter of sequential single-digit growth.

However, a sharp rise in the cost of finished goods purchases and employee expenses, both growing in double digits for the second consecutive quarter, has impacted profitability. Net profit for the December quarter recorded a modest 1.5 percent year-on-year increase, the slowest growth in six quarters, while sequentially, net profit declined for the second consecutive quarter. Only those firms that had comparable data for 15 previous quarters were chosen.

For the BSE MidCap Index, Moneycontrol has aggregate data for 85 firms. Despite a 12.9 percent rise in revenue for Q3, the highest in eight quarters, net profit increased by only 2 percent, marking the slowest growth in six quarters. The subdued profit growth, despite higher revenue, was attributed to rising total expenses and interest costs. Total costs surged by 11 percent, the steepest increase in six quarters, while interest costs soared over 27 percent, the highest jump in three quarters.

Catch all the market action on our live blog

Energy, banking and financial services companies were excluded from the study.

The weak earnings in the mid-cap and small-cap space can be attributed to poor demand, and inflationary pressures which directly affected consumer spending. Also, global uncertainties, rising input costs, and higher borrowing costs strained the bottom line figures. The growing premiumization trend has also affected smaller companies which are still at the nascent stage as compared to large counterparts, experts said.

Mayank Mundhra, FRM- VP Risk & Head Research Abans Group said weaker-than-expected growth—driven by sluggish global demand from key trade partners like the US and Europe—led to margin compression. This slowdown was further intensified by the 2024 election cycle, as markets anticipated potential shifts in political power globally, leading to cautious spending and weaker demand.

Sectors like capital goods, industrials, and consumer discretionary were hit hardest, struggling with weak demand and high capital needs. Rising borrowing costs further pressured profits despite revenue growth.

Experts say small and midcap margins are lower and more cyclical than large caps due to their growth focus. Post-COVID, they saw stronger margin gains, but with tailwinds fading, pressure is mounting. While consensus expects SMID profits to outperform in FY26, weak revenue and shrinking margins put this at risk. Continued underperformance could threaten their valuation premiums.

Motilal Oswal’s latest note highlights that the recent correction in broader markets has accounted for some potential earnings disappointments ahead. However, mid and small-cap valuations remain elevated compared to their historical averages and relative to the Nifty 50. The Nifty is currently trading at a 12-month forward P/E of 19.3x, below its long-term average of 20.5x. Given this backdrop, the brokerage maintains a preference for large-cap stocks.

Narinder Wadhwa, Managing Director & CEO of SKI capital services said key risks persist, including double-digit growth in employee costs, which could offset potential cost savings, and a weak demand environment that may constrain earnings growth. Additionally, global economic factors such as tariff wars and the likelihood of no Fed rate cuts further add to the uncertainty.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: BASF Plans New Mid-double-digit Million-euro Alcoholates Plant in Germany
data.description:
BASF disclosed on Wednesday that it is investing a mid-double-digit million-euro amount for the development of a new alcoholates plant at its site in Ludwigshafen, Germany.

The new plant will replace the German chemicals group's existing production facility in the city, with startup expected in the second half of 2027.

The plant will produce sodium methylate and potassium methylate, which are used for biodiesel production, as well as for pharmaceutical and agricultural applications.

data.title: Lenovo Doubles Profit on Strong Sales
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Lenovo Group reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings, with device sales strong, boosted by its bet on the fast-growing use of artificial intelligence.

Net profit for the three months ended December more than doubled to $693 million, the world's largest PC maker said Thursday, beating the $381.7 million estimate in a FactSet poll of analysts. The result was partly boosted by a $282 million nonrecurring income tax credit.

Revenue was $18.80 billion, up 20% from a year earlier, also topping analysts' estimates. Lenovo's main business, the intelligent-devices segment, which includes PCs, smartphones and tablets, delivered a 12% revenue gain.

The results reflect a solid commercial PC replacement cycle but also show that the company is reaping the benefits after launching several AI PC models in China last May.

Lenovo Chief Executive Yang Yuanqing told The Wall Street Journal in November that AI PCs could make up 25% of its global PC shipments this year and reach 70%-80% by 2027 as the premium product becomes mainstream.

The company's Hong Kong-listed shares have risen more than 25% so far this year, driven in part by investors' exuberance over Chinese AI startup DeepSeek's breakthrough in January.

Lenovo has been touted as a key beneficiary of the tech advancement. Analysts at UOB said that cheaper, more accessible AI features introduced by DeepSeek could encourage more enterprises, especially small and midsize companies, to invest in AI equipment, which may accelerate replacement demand for PCs and servers.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

New Zealand Shares Fall 1.2% on Broad-Based Weakness — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 closed 1.2% lower at 12880.36, dragged to its largest one-day fall in more than two weeks from broad-based weakness. Shares of utilities, aged-care providers, property developers and consumer-related companies all finished lower as investors digested a raft of earnings reports and the RBNZ's move to cut the country's cash rate on Wednesday. Casino operator SkyCity led losses, tumbling 7.5% after releasing its December-half results. Local shares of Australian banks Westpac and ANZ lost 5.0% and 4.1%, respectively, mirroring falls in their domestic markets. Precinct Properties shed 2.0% following the release of its interim results. The NZX-50 is down 0.8% so far this week and on course for a sixth weekly loss in eight weeks. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: India 10-year yield holds at key level ahead of RBI's bond auction
data.description:
Indian government bond yields were largely unchanged on Thursday, with the benchmark bond yield near the key technical level of 6.68%, with the focus on the central bank's second open market purchase of bonds in as many weeks.

The benchmark 10-year yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.6820% as of 10:00 a.m. IST, compared with its previous close of 6.6859%.

The Reserve Bank of India will buy bonds worth up to 400 billion rupees ($4.61 billion) later in the day, the last leg of its mega liquidity infusion package announced in late January.

"The cutoffs would be the key deciding factor whether the benchmark bond yield will be able to break 6.68% on the downside or will reverse one more time," a trader with a primary dealership said.

The benchmark bond is part of the upcoming purchase. The RBI bought around 50 billion rupees of this paper in its first debt purchase but did not include it in the second auction.

Over the last five weeks, the central bank has bought bonds worth 600 billion rupees via auctions and another 388.15 billion rupees through secondary market screen-based purchases.

The 10-year bond yield has eased nearly 20 basis points during this period from a high of 6.87% hit on January 14.

Apart from bond buying, the RBI has also injected around 440 billion rupees through a dollar/rupee buy/sell swap and 1.82 trillion rupees through four-day to 56-day repos, as well as provided funds through daily overnight repos.

Traders also await New Delhi's penultimate auction for the current financial year and the minutes of RBI's last meeting on Friday.

Meanwhile, the minutes of the Federal Reserve's January meeting showed that U.S. President Donald Trump's initial policy proposals raised concerns about higher inflation.

($1 = 86.8170 Indian rupees)

data.title: Courage Investment Forecasts Profit for 2024
data.description: Courage Investment is expecting to book a profit of no less than $1.6 million for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, compared with a loss of around $4.0 a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

data.title: Metaplanet Now Holds 2,100 Bitcoin, Purchases 68 More BTC
data.description:
Metaplanet (3350) has now accumulated 2,100 bitcoin (BTC), now holding 0.01% of the total BTC supply that will ever be mined, which is 21 million BTC. Metaplanet purchased 68.59 BTC for $6.6 million at an average purchase price of $96,335 per bitcoin.

According to Dylan Le Clair, Metaplanet has raised $20 million in equity capital in the first two trading days of its "21 million plan". Metaplanet also announced on Feb. 18 that they will execute a 10-1 stock split on April 1. This comes just eight months after a reverse a 1-for-10 reverse split.

Metaplanet shares are up over 1% in the current market trading; shares are trading at 6,260 JPY.

data.title: Dubai Taxi's FY24 Profit Slips; Revenue Up
data.description:
Dubai Taxi Co. (DFM:DTC) said Wednesday that its profit for full-year 2024 fell, while revenue from contracts with customers rose year over year.

Profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 331.3 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 345.3 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.13 dirham from 0.14 dirham.

The Dubai-listed taxi services company's revenue from contracts with customers was 2.20 billion dirhams, compared with 1.95 billion dirhams earlier.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a final dividend of 0.0489 dirham per share for the second half of 2024, payable in April 2025, subject to approval at a general assembly. The final dividend brings the total dividend amount for full-year 2024 to 0.1126 dirham per share.

data.title: Moody’s upgrades credit outlook for LG Electronics to ‘positive’
data.description: Moody’s Investors Service has upgraded LG Electronics’ credit outlook from “stable” to “positive,” the first such revision since 2021. The company’s credit rating remains at Baa2, Moody’s announced on Wednesday. Despite economic uncertainties, including rising tariffs and sluggish demand for certain products, Moody’s said that LG Electronics is expected to maintain financial stability through steady profitability and sound financial indicators. A key factor in the outlook upgrade was the credit improvement of LG Display, a subsidiary in which LG Electronics holds a 36.72 percent stake. Moody’s noted that LG Display’s improving financial position reduces the risk of contingent support from LG Electronics while also enhancing its equity method earnings. Moody’s highlighted LG Electronics’ global brand strength, market leadership, business diversification, and geographic expansion as key credit-supporting factors. It also pointed out that although it was not factored into this rating adjustment, LG Electronics’ ongoing initial public offering (IPO) of its Indian subsidiary could further strengthen its financial standing. LG Electronics filed a preliminary listing application with India’s Securities and Exchange Board at the end of 2024 and aims to sell a 15 percent stake in its Indian subsidiary via the IPO.
